title: Extend abstraction projection to zones
description: Allow the existing abstraction slider/projection to collapse zone hierarchies as well as subgraphs, so physical areas can be viewed as representative components with rewired and deduplicated external edges.
decision_notes: Added `abstractionTarget: "subgraph" | "zone"` while preserving `subgraph` as the default for compatibility.
Zone abstraction removes the selected zone's area box and replaces it with a representative component. Parent zones/boundaries then refer to that representative as a node.
The same projection path rewires external edges, deduplicates repeated source/target pairs, and rewrites data, identity, permission, diagnostic, zone, and boundary references before layout/render.
Playground/static demo and `<archmap-viewer controls>` now expose an Abstraction target selector plus depth slider.
notes: Browser automation connection timed out while reconnecting after this change, so visual browser confirmation was not completed in this turn. Local dev server listeners on port 4174 remained present.
Future polish: decide whether zone abstraction nodes should have a distinct visual style from ordinary components.
